_doc: Companies that should move TO 'Beauty & Personal Care' from other cats (Consumer Goods / Retail / Healthcare / Food & Beverage). Built 2026-06-08 from `wiki.industry` matches on /cosmetic|beauty|personal care|fragran|haircare|hair care|perfume|skincare|skin care|toiletries/ AND brand-name matches against a hand-curated list of known beauty/PC brands. Includes pure-play brands and dedicated personal-care divisions. EXCLUDES diversified consumer conglomerates (Procter & Gamble, Unilever, Colgate-Palmolive, Kimberly-Clark, Newell Brands, Edgewell, Church & Dwight, International Flavors & Fragrances, Coty's holding) — these stay in Consumer Goods because they sell food/household/etc. too and a single cat is reductive. EXCLUDES Sephora and Ulta (those are RETAILERS — keep in Retail; they sell beauty but aren't a beauty brand). EXCLUDES household-care SKUs (Bounty paper, Dawn dish soap, Febreze, Tide, Swiffer, Charmin, Pampers, Always, Tampax) — those are home-care/feminine-care, not beauty. INCLUDES dental/oral care brands (Crest, Listerine, Colgate-the-toothpaste-brand, Sensodyne) — these ARE personal care. Goal: get Beauty & Personal Care from 17 → 150+. Final landed count after this list applies: ~75 (was unable to safely classify more without manual brand-by-brand review — many Consumer Goods entries are sub-brands of conglomerates whose detail pages give no industry signal).
